summary: Six changes ordered by dependency. Two independent roots: c001 (pg_cron amd64 link) and c002 (realtime fail-closed) share no files and can run in parallel. c001 is the taproot — it unblocks c003 and c006 and costs zero waits, so it goes first. c003 (green integration CI) is the wait-budget risk: 0 successes in 8 runs and nobody has seen the job past its build step, so its failures are unknowable and were deliberately not pre-specced. c004 and c006 are BLOCKED on human decisions and are ordered late so they do not stall Tier-0 work. Wait budget allocated at 3 (the documented per-epoch limit); p15 overran at 6. No release change is specced because the re-tag-vs-v1.0.1 question remains unanswered and speccing one would presuppose the answer.
backend_note: Pinned in project.json this stage. The skill's own detection rule (openspec/ exists -> emit /opsx:new) conflicted with decision-log D-002, which chose native. Emitting /opsx:new would have created a SECOND competing task surface for the same six changes — precisely the divergence that broke p15. Verified `kbd-apply list <change>` reads the native tasks.json correctly, so the native surface is walkable.
library_candidates_note: library-candidates.json has zero candidates by design; all six changes are build changes with no `library:` annotation. OpenSSF CVD guide adopted as a template for c004, not as a dependency.

order.note: parallel with c001; BREAKING per Base Rule #16

wait_budget.rationale: Documented per-epoch budget (Integration-First policy). p15 overran at 6, recorded as debt in p15/handoffs/reflect.json.
wait_budget.strategy: Spend at genuine integration checkpoints, not on validating individual functions. Do not wait on c002's integration test in isolation — land c001, land c002, then take one wait on c003 that validates image + migrations + subscription path together.
wait_budget.escalation: If the budget reaches 3 with c003 still red: HALT and surface it. Do not silently overrun as p15 did.

blocked_changes.blocked_on: Human must name the vulnerability-report recipient. Can start the instant this clears, in parallel with any other change.
Human must confirm whether a backup/restore runbook exists outside this repository. If yes: doc link plus verification. If no: days of engineering.

open_questions_carried: BLOCKING RELEASE: Re-tag v1.0.0 or ship v1.0.1? Now unresolved across four stages (assess, analyze, spec, plan). v1.0.0 is public with an arm64-only image and 41 commits have landed since.
Does the beta include realtime subscriptions? c002 assumes yes.
Is v0.10.0 -> v1.0.0 a supported upgrade? p35-c004 landed a BREAKING PgBackend::acquire change.
stale_state_found: current-waypoint.md still described p15 as in_progress with 0/5 changes done — rewritten this stage.
position-reminder.txt caches changes_total from current-waypoint.json and read 0/4 after spec produced six changes; corrected by refreshing the waypoint.
next_action: /kbd-apply p16-c001-pgcron-amd64-link — 5 tasks, no deps, no wait cost, unblocks half the phase.
